title: ajax cyclamineus
intro: Ajax cyclamineus is the botanical name for a type of wild daffodil plant, specifically the Narcissus pseudonarcissus var. cyclamineus, which is a perennial flowering plant native to Europe. The plant's medicinal properties include its ability to induce vomiting and act as a purgative, making it traditionally used to treat various ailments such as indigestion, fever, and rheumatism. The health benefits of Ajax cyclamineus are attributed to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe skin irritations, reduce swelling, and alleviate respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of the plant include tinctures, teas, and infusions, which can be used to make topical creams and ointments for skin conditions or ingested as a medicinal draught to aid in digestion and relieve symptoms of colds and flu.
